vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-485: Add workspace lifecycle events to fix stale UI after wipe/cleanup/abort

plan.title: Add workspace lifecycle events to fix stale UI after wipe/cleanup/abort
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-6

narratives.Problem: Workspace operations (wipe, cleanup, abort, start-planning) don't emit domain events, so the frontend stays stale until tracker poll fires (1-3s delay).
narratives.Proposal: Define 5 new workspace lifecycle events in contracts, emit them in the 4 affected routes, and add reducer cases. The shared reducer pattern means the frontend reacts automatically — no frontend code changes needed.

items.title: Define workspace lifecycle event schemas in contracts

narrative.Action: Add 5 new event schemas (WorkspaceCreatedEvent, WorkspaceWipeStartedEvent, WorkspaceDestroyedEvent, WorkspaceDeletedEvent, WorkspaceAbortedEvent) to packages/contracts/src/events.ts following the existing pattern. Add all 5 to the DomainEvent union.

subItems.title: 5 new event schemas exist in events.ts with correct type literals and payload shapes
All 5 events are included in the DomainEvent union
contracts package builds cleanly with npm run build

items.title: Add reducer cases for all workspace lifecycle events

narrative.Action: Add switch cases in applyEvent() for workspace.created (no-op), workspace.wipe_started (set wiping state on issue), workspace.destroyed (remove agents, reset to todo), workspace.deleted (same as destroyed), workspace.aborted (remove planning agent). The exhaustive switch ensures TypeScript catches missing cases.

subItems.title: workspace.destroyed and workspace.deleted remove agent entries and reset canonicalStatus to todo
workspace.wipe_started sets a wiping transitional state on the issue
workspace.aborted removes the planning agent from agentsById
TypeScript exhaustive check passes (no compile errors)

items.title: Emit workspace events in the 4 affected route handlers

narrative.Action: In issues.ts routes: (1) start-planning: emit workspace.created after worktree setup, before planning.started. (2) abort-planning: emit workspace.aborted after tmux kill-session. (3) cleanup-workspace: emit workspace.deleted after successful cleanup. (4) deep-wipe: emit workspace.wipe_started at start, workspace.destroyed at end. Use existing eventStore.append() pattern via Effect DI.

subItems.title: start-planning emits workspace.created after worktree creation
deep-wipe emits workspace.wipe_started immediately and workspace.destroyed on completion
abort-planning emits workspace.aborted after session kill
All new events appear in the event store audit log (SQLite events table)

items.title: Pass all quality gates (typecheck, lint, test)

narrative.Action: Run npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test. Fix any issues. Rebuild contracts package first since server depends on it.
